message:
fix(database/mysql): fail-closed protocol desync prevention on cancellation

Move `self.inner.closed = false` from the blanket post-read position to
individual success paths (OK packet, result set completion, server ERR
with valid error structure). This ensures that if a future is dropped
mid-protocol-exchange — e.g. by timeout, select!, or cancellation — the
connection stays closed and is not returned to the pool in a desynced
state.

Specifically:
- query(): closed=false only on OK packet, completed result set, or
  well-formed server ERR; malformed/empty responses stay fail-closed
- execute(): same pattern — closed=false on OK with affected_rows,
  completed result set, or well-formed server ERR only
- Cancelled outcomes never re-open the connection

Adds test infrastructure (NoopWaker, poll_once, make_command_connection_
with_single_response) and three new regression tests:
- malformed_server_err_packet_keeps_query_connection_closed
- malformed_server_err_packet_keeps_execute_connection_closed
- dropped_result_set_query_keeps_connection_closed (verifies that
  dropping a query future mid-result-set keeps the connection fail-closed
  by polling until the server's first result-set packet is consumed,
  then dropping the future)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
